title: MedObvious: Exposing the Medical Moravec’s Paradox in VLMs via Clinical Triage

section_anchors.text: We introduce MedObvious, a 1,880-task benchmark that isolates input validation as a set-level consistency capability over small multi-panel image sets
These results show that pre-diagnostic verification remains unsolved for medical VLMs
Clinical interpretation begins with pre-diagnostic triage
We introduce MedObvious, a benchmark for pre-diagnostic visual sanity checking in medical images
MedObvious is a benchmark designed to test whether medical VLMs can recognize obvious input-level inconsistencies before attempting any diagnosis
Each MedObvious instance presents a grid of n images
MedObvious targets a pre-diagnostic requirement, i.e., before interpretation, the model must verify that the input is coherent and safe to reason over
We present MedObvious, a benchmark for pre-diagnostic visual sanity checking in medical VLMs
current models remain unreliable gatekeepers, with frequent false alarms, scaling degradation, and strong format sensitivity
